atomicity.message: The staged diff is one bug fix: ci --commit now honors explicit --feature when auditing committed multi-cluster payloads, with focused regression coverage and tracker state for the blocker bead.

conditions.message: Staged CI fix passes formatting and focused/full Go checks.
evidence: go run ./cmd/burpvalve hash --staged --json matched staged hash sha256:e2332d370b939efb9f151dba4ec8b9b83277ac904c587208482cf09e95878135 and included exactly .beads/issues.jsonl, cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go, internal/backpressure/ci.go.
git diff --cached --check exited 0.
go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'TestCICommitExplicitFeatureAuditsMultiClusterCommit' -count=1 passed.
go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Test.*CI|TestDetectFeaturesExplicitFlag' -count=1 passed.
go test ./... passed for all packages.

conditions.message: The change removes the commit-mode special case instead of adding a parallel audit path; the new fixture/test reuses existing helpers and keeps duplication local to the regression setup.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status showed exactly .beads/issues.jsonl, cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go, and internal/backpressure/ci.go staged.

git diff --cached -- internal/backpressure/ci.go cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go showed production code only changes explicitFeatureForPlan to return opts.ExplicitFeature; existing assertCommitFeature remains in RunCI.
rg found one production helper, the existing assertion helper, and one focused regression fixture/test.

verifier.transcript_ref: Agent Mail message 3006 / thread project-readiness-kit-ci-commit-multicluster-feature-9hf; ScarletMarsh corroborated in message 3005

conditions.message: The fix does not turn --feature into a way to launder arbitrary attestations: matching multi-cluster commit audits now pass, but mismatched feature assertions still block against the committed attestation feature id.
evidence: go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit f3c4a46 --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-option-b-install-surfaces-idu returned status passed and feature_id project-readiness-kit-oxp-option-b-install-surfaces-idu for the real multi-cluster OXP option-B commit.

go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit HEAD --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-skill-refactor-prompt-bank-fy8 returned status passed and feature_id project-readiness-kit-oxp-skill-refactor-prompt-bank-fy8 for the real multi-cluster skill-refactor commit.
go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit f3c4a46 --feature wrong-feature exited nonzero with feature assertion mismatch.
ScarletMarsh independently verified the matching pass, wrong-feature failure, and regression test coverage.

conditions.message: The changed behavior has direct regression coverage for a committed multi-cluster payload with matching --feature and a wrong-feature assertion failure, while existing commit-audit tests remain covered.
evidence: git diff --cached -- cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go added TestCICommitExplicitFeatureAuditsMultiClusterCommit, which creates a committed payload spanning src/app.go and docs/readme.md, expects ci --commit <sha> --feature br-123 to pass, and expects wrong-feature to fail with feature assertion mismatch.
go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'TestCICommitAuditsSpecifiedCommit|TestCICommitExplicitFeatureAuditsMultiClusterCommit' -count=1 passed.
go test ./internal/backpressure -run 'Test.*CI|TestDetectFeaturesExplicitFlag' -count=1 passed.

evidence: go run ./cmd/burpvalve hash --staged --json reported sha256:e2332d370b939efb9f151dba4ec8b9b83277ac904c587208482cf09e95878135, matching the packet.

br show project-readiness-kit-ci-commit-multicluster-feature-9hf showed acceptance criteria for multi-cluster pass with matching --feature, wrong-feature mismatch, preserving single-cluster behavior and robot mode, focused tests, and go test ./....

go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit f3c4a46 --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-option-b-install-surfaces-idu passed; go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit HEAD --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-skill-refactor-prompt-bank-fy8 passed; wrong-feature failed with feature assertion mismatch; go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit 1ead769 --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-hash-staged-helper-a48 passed as single-cluster control.
go test ./... passed all packages.

conditions.message: The committed attestation binding remains intact: explicit feature scopes the expected plan, but validation still rejects an attestation whose stored feature id does not match the assertion, and artifact paths/payload hashes are preserved in robot output.
evidence: go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit f3c4a46 --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-option-b-install-surfaces-idu returned artifact_path backpressure/attestations/66154fa9880b4acce1a5af4fa4484e2e2235d8daa904f30efd61e6ef89966237.json, feature_id project-readiness-kit-oxp-option-b-install-surfaces-idu, and the expected .beads/cmd/internal payload paths.

wrong-feature command against f3c4a46 returned the committed attestation feature id in the mismatch message instead of passing or silently selecting another feature.

conditions.message: No new external, secret, permission, or execution boundary is introduced; this is a local CI audit path change, and the safety boundary around asserted feature identity remains enforced.
evidence: git diff --cached -- internal/backpressure/ci.go showed no new IO, network, shell execution, secret handling, or authorization surface; it only returns opts.ExplicitFeature for plan creation.
rg scan found assertCommitFeature still called in RunCI and still returning feature assertion mismatch when explicit feature differs from the committed attestation feature.
The live wrong-feature audit exited nonzero with feature assertion mismatch, confirming the identity boundary is enforced after the plan is scoped.

ScarletMarsh independently verified no auth, network, file permission, hook execution, shell invocation, verifier provenance, or attestation trust-boundary code is changed outside internal/backpressure/ci.go.

conditions.message: No visual or TUI surface exists in this staged payload.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status shows only .beads/issues.jsonl, cmd/burpvalve/commit_test.go, and internal/backpressure/ci.go.
No UI/TUI rendering, screenshots, SVG/GIF assets, terminal styling, or visual output files are staged.

conditions.message: The runtime change is constant-time option plumbing and the full suite passed.
evidence: Inspected internal/backpressure/ci.go: the staged runtime change removes three lines so explicitFeatureForPlan returns opts.ExplicitFeature for commit audits instead of blanking it.
No loops, external calls, broad scans, concurrency, or algorithmic expansion are added in runtime code.
go test ./... passed, including cmd/burpvalve and internal/backpressure.

conditions.message: The fix keeps ci read-only and preserves the user's required explicit assertion contract: agents can audit historical multi-cluster commits, but cannot use --feature to self-certify a mismatched attestation.
evidence: Packet and staged diff identify only CI audit code/test/tracker changes; no commit, push, staging, mail, pane, or workflow-engine behavior is added.
go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit f3c4a46 --feature project-readiness-kit-oxp-option-b-install-surfaces-idu passed in robot/read-only audit mode.

go run ./cmd/burpvalve --robots ci --commit f3c4a46 --feature wrong-feature failed with feature assertion mismatch, so explicit --feature remains an assertion that requires the committed evidence to agree.
